description: Workspace, section and domain model for supporting multiple work scopes without forking the intelligence pipeline.
core_rule: Every workspace keeps the core intelligence modules: source management, candidate pool, daily reports, weekly reports, historical archive, entity timeline archive, quality archive, SQL exports, users and audit. Optional modules are database-registered and disabled by default.

workspace.purpose: A user-facing work scope that still uses the same intelligence pipeline.
owns: isolated unified label policy for model tagging

section.purpose: A database-registered core page or optional plugin page inside a workspace.

domain.purpose: The subject matter of intelligence content, such as AI, hardware, semiconductor, robotics or policy.

fields: code (^[a-z][a-z0-9_]*$, unique across enabled and disabled workspaces)
provisioning: All core_sections are registered and enabled for the new workspace.
config_json.label_policy starts from the default ai_sql_categories policy and is then edited per workspace via PATCH /api/workspaces/{code}/label-policy.
Existing super admins receive owner memberships.
sort_order is appended after existing workspaces.
rules: Custom workspaces are database records; the startup seed only maintains the built-in planning_intel and ai_tools definitions and must never disable or overwrite custom workspaces.
New workspaces integrate sources through the shared pool: enable existing sources via workspace_source_links or create new ones via POST /api/sources.
Creating a workspace must not fork the pipeline, the SQL contract or the taxonomy files.

section_groups.notes: Navigation is grouped vertically by editorial job. The group lives in workspace_sections.config_json.group and is returned by the sections API; the frontend renders groups dynamically.

scope_fields.workspace_code: Which workspace a processed object belongs to. Data source definitions are reusable and are enabled through workspace_source_links.
scope_fields.domain_code: Which intelligence domain the content belongs to.
scope_fields.visibility_scope: Who can see or sync the object.
scope_fields.sync_policy: How the object moves between public, intranet and local deployments.

rules: Do not use domain_code as a UI workspace boundary.
Do not create a separate frontend/backend for any workspace.
Do not remove source management, candidate pool, daily reports or weekly reports from any workspace.
Do not duplicate data source definitions per workspace; reuse data_sources through workspace_source_links.
New work scopes are workspaces; optional pages/tools are workspace_sections with enabled=false by default.
The frontend must load workspace list and enabled sections from backend APIs; hard-coded navigation is only a temporary scaffold.
Do not show tool_catalog, tool_runs or standalone_topics unless workspace_sections explicitly enables them.
First-level and second-level news labels are configured once per workspace in workspaces.config_json.label_policy and constrain model-generated news categories.

planning_intel defaults to ai_sql_categories from config/taxonomy/news_categories.json. ai_tools defaults to ai_tools_categories with 工具新功能/工具新案例/工具新技术 and cursor/claude code/opencode/codex as second-level labels under each primary label.
Source-side tags use planning_source_tags from config/taxonomy/source_tags.json and are only data-source metadata/hints; they must not become generated_news.category.
First-level and second-level labels are the directory for clustering and recommendation; do not add a separate tool management page for that.
Data APIs that list reusable sources may filter by workspace_code through workspace_source_links.
Workspace membership controls access to a desktop, while global roles still control privileged actions.
